name: Emulator recovers transparently from a mid-session drop
hypothesis: A connection dropped mid-exchange (relay reset, gateway restart) must not wedge the aggregator: the next operation should transparently re-establish the session and succeed, with no manual reconnect step — late/dropped requests are harmless because the emulator redials and retries.
expected: After the session is dropped mid-exchange, the very next data operation transparently re-establishes the session and succeeds — the emulator survives the drop without an explicit resume.
fix: If the emulator never recovers, look at Conn.ensureConn/redial (the single immediate redial on a broken session) and the callT exception-vs-transport classification (only a transport break marks the session broken; a protocol exception must NOT).
notes: Distinct from resumption-after-drop: this asserts RECOVERY (sessionSurvival), not whether the recovered session resumed. There is deliberately no resume step — the follow-up discover redials transparently. Loopback-only (desktop, T06.8).
